commentBody: i thought the instructions were novel so i followed them precisely. the salmon was way under.  i would have been better off to simply boil the rice, roast the salmon, do the add-ons and make a bowl.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: On advice from other commentators, I used way more seasoning for the salmon than the recipe called for, and added some lemon juice to the yogurt sauce instead of water. I just had some of the leftovers for lunch, cold, and I actually enjoyed it much more! I did over salt it, but that's a me issue, not the recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Is sockeye salmon too lean for this dish?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I thought it was quite bland. I would consider making it again but way more spice on the salmon.  I didn’t have an issue with the salmon cooking but I did dice mine quite small.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I use most recipes as suggestions. I didn't have a large dutch oven so cooked dish in a deep frying pan. Didn't have the lime so used Roses Lime Juice which added the necessary sweetness. Frying pan had a glass cover so it was easy to see when salmon was done. Crushed coriander seed on salmon was DELICIOUS.  Sauce was great. Made a mango inflected salad with Chinese cabbage and will add mango to the dish next time. I think any firm fish will do just fine. May try tuna or cod next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: @Alana You could also cook the rice in fish broth next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Very easy and pretty yummy. Yogurt sauce is very good.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I also had raw fish after 12 minutes.  I had turned the flame to low and put a flame diffuser under the pot.  Ended up adding more water and cooking at higher heat for another 10-12 minutes. Wound up with soggy rice but it was good.  I think that the best solution might be to add the fish and peas just when you turn the rice down after it boils.  Also a little more water might be needed to get the rice soft.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Pretty good, and quick. Hlf this recipe makes 2 good dinners.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Followed the instructions exact.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: It was good.  Salmon needed more time to cook.  The rice was very boring.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I kept the flame between 1-2 setting and kept an eye on the fish through a clear lidded pot until it was no longer shiny/raw, definitely a bit longer than 10 minutes. The yogurt sauce is integral to the flavor of this dish! I added freshly squeezed lemon juice and some dill to the sauce. Next time, I will add more than 1/2 cup of frozen peas.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: This one was a bust. Salmon had no flavor, dill overpowered the rice flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: This is the first recipe on NYT Cooking that we actively did not like and we’ve made hundreds at this point. I would skip this one.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Thank you for this!  I cooked it a bit longer and it was perfect. So easy and refreshing for hot weather- I will be cooking this all summer. The coriander and the yogurt sauce made it really delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Salmon needs longer to cook. Rice needs seasoning. Visually appealing but flawed recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: This had literally no taste whatsoever

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: salmon looks more raw than cooked in the pic, not sure why folks thought it would turn out otherwise.In the past I would look at a pic of a recipe and the ingredients and try it,only going to the comments during or after. Now I never try a recipe without reading the comments first. I always find good suggestions and clarifications in the comments. This is the first time I've come across a recipe where a supermajority of the commenters not only changed it but disliked it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I pan seared my salmon - did not cook through with rice. Added some shallot to yogurt/ cucumber. Served more thinly sliced cucumber with it. Delicious!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I always do 2 to 1 ratio, liquid to rice, with Basmati rice - My standard is 2 cups salt free chicken stock to 1 cup of rice, so with 1.5C of rice, 3C of water/stock would be a better ratio.  And, it usually takes about 35 to 40 minutes to cook the rice until all the liquid is absorbed.  This recipe needs some tweaking.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and we like salmon medium, so 10 minutes was fine.  However, I followed the recipe and there’s WAY too much rice.  I would use 1/2 cup and double the peas.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody:
Okay, after reading 57 comments - most of them negative- I decided to prove most of you wrong by adding salt & pepper to the rice, adding honey & ginger to the salmon, and extending the cooking time to avoid sushi. Oh, and I added capers to the tzatziki sauce. 

Y’all were right. I just can’t get excited about this recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Basically make each component (salmon / rice & peas / cucumber salad) fully separate and you’ll be successful at the end ;-)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: Any other option other than Dutch oven?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I used a rice cooker. But I also pan fried the salmon!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I loved this! After reading other's comments, I decided to pan fry my salmon, especially since it was skin-on. I also used fresh peas from my garden. Really delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody:
This was good. I had the fish monger cut the salmon in chunks. That made all of the difference. I also used microwaveable jasmine rice. That cut the time so I could sauté the salmon without it being overcooked. 
Then just added the peas, and some soy sauce. I then made the cucumber mixture. The family loved it

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: One-Pot Salmon and Dill Rice With Peas Recipe
commentBody: I agree with some of the other comments. I had to cook the salmon about 5 minutes longer. I found the dish rather bland. I wouldn’t make it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Super delicious and gobbled up by Indian husband and two littles. So quick and easy for a week night meal. The spices are common to Indian cuisine so we had them on hand already. 
We love exposing our children to different cuisines and flavors to widen their palate and world view and teaches them to be respectful of other cultures.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @himself a well stocked pantry pays dividends on weeknights

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
I have spent a lot of time making biryanis over the years. This recipe results in an excellent version in a quarter of the time (assuming you have the spices on hand). 

Small changes I made:
- I used ground lamb as this is closer to my old fashioned lamb biryani. 
- I doubled the spices. I’ll admit to loving spice-filled biryanis. 
- I doubled the garlic and ginger.  To save a lot of time, you can buy garlic / ginger paste from Amazon (I like Rani brand). It keeps forever and is useful in many recipes. 
- it’s an extravagance, but to add a little crumbled roasted saffron in the milk that’s poured over the rice at the end makes it even more authentic

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Rick in SF 

I like your idea of using lamb, and I thought the dish was great but would probably double the spices next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Rick in SF is the garlic /ginger the combo option Rani makes?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
This was lovely.  Cut recipe in half because there are just two of us but kept spices for full recipe.
Changes I made beside the spices were to infuse the milk with some saffron treads  ( warm the milk and  add 1/4 teaspoon or so of threads and let sit for 10 min)
I also sauted some Chopped cashews and currents ( you could use raisins) in some ghee until well browned/reddish and added it to the top before serving

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Could the beef be subbed with chicken or turkey? My gf doesn’t eat beef or pork.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Nick K same here. Anyone tried w chicken?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I'm going to try turkey.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Nick K IMO, yes; get dark meat if you can.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is a delicious albeit time consuming recipe. I say time consuming because it’s not exactly week night simple, as stated. I used very lean ground beef which was fine but I’d do the 80% next time. Not having ghee, I used melted butter and it was perfect. The six cardamom pods can be subbed with a scant tsp of ground cardamom. I used the 1/4tsp cayenne and it was plenty hot. Cilantro is a no-go for us so didn’t use it. It’s a fabulous recipe and the flavors are a great combination. Worth the effort.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Can I use ground lamb to miake this?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I would think that would make it even more flavorful!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Categrace I think lamb would be much better.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This would be more closer to a pulao than biryani as biryani means layered rice dish! :) Lovely recipe, though!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Nidhi this was my exact thought. Pulao or keema rice as my family called it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
A great recipe, incredible flavours!
As usual, timing is off.  Two minutes not enough to soften the onions; count on 4.  Three minutes to brown the beef so there's no pink showing isn't possible; took 8-10 minutes.  So the rice is cooked too early and sits waiting/drying.  Start the onions 4-5  before the rice, and you're good.
To be clear, once rice boils, simmer with lid off.
Finally:  it says to fluff rice at end...you can't!  It's underneath the beef and onions!!
Again, great flavours!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Delicious comfort food

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Frankly, I am deterred by the number of ingredients, dried or otherwise. Yeesh

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Gina Some dishes require a little more time in the kitchen, usually to better effect.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Gina Well worth the time. I assembled all the dry ingredients the day before, so it was easy. Dried fennel was an odd one, but I had fennel seeds which I ground and included.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Ground meat is never used for biryani and rice is layered with meat or just a layer on top…
Biryani is such an iconic dish that it needs to be at least somewhat similar to the real thing
It’s like saying I’m serving up roast turkey and then serving turkey burgers for Thanksgiving…just to give folks an idea of where I’m coming from 
I’m open to variations usually but the ground meat part deters me in this…it’s more of a pulao

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Oh, no, not at all!

Keema biryani is made with ground meat, by definition!  Keema biryani is very popular in India, so please don't say that using ground meat makes it not biryani!  It goes all the way back to the Mughal era!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Squirrel Butler we can agree to disagree…hard to really verify these things one way or another. I’m sure one can get keema “biryani” or pulao (more accurate) all over India and it might be a nice variation on the real thing. But the genuine article it isn’t….

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Squirrel Butler I noticed that the intro of the recipe does mention that this is a “cheater“ version of biryani, which is quick and easy to make. So yes, I agree with that and should have read it before posting my comment. 
But no household in Lucknow or Hyderabad is going to invite you over for a biryani meal and serve Keema biryani. It might be served as a variation or be on restaurant menus, sure…

author: Kay Chun

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe

Stir in yogurt off heat once browned. Use the beef mixture plus some milk to top par-cooked rice. Finish in oven."

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @judith  Why not measure and combine the spices (excluding salt and pepper).  Store them all in a glass container and you’re good to go whenever.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Andrea
Thank you! 🙏 I am a full time caregiver for my ill husband. Wondrous idea. Will share with others in a similar situation. 💕⭐️

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I realize it’s probably not traditional, but I added peas and chopped red bell pepper and it was amazing!  Plus the extra pops of color added to the presentation nicely.

@Tim
I would consider adding baby spinach at the end to make it a complete one-pot meal. Well, two pots to sauté the onions and meat. But the baby spinach wouldn’t change the flavor much and would add the necessary veg. But I like your ideas of red bell pepper and peas as well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Biryani’s has meat in the bottom and rice on the top.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Akbar depends where you are… growing up, my mom’s was always a mix-and-bake.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I will cook this tonight. It sounds amazing. Thanks for the heads up about most of the ingredients being spices. Long lists do intimidate me, but I try to not let them stop me from discovering more deliciousness.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Ok, should add ghee to the pantry at some point, might as well be now. Maybe ground fennel, but luckily I can stop by the co-op and get a Tbs or so for a few pennies. Everything else is buried in the cabinet somewhere - I can dig it out. Cilantro is a dividing factor in the house, so I'll give it a shot without, maybe with parsley sub. Looking at the directions, the biggest thing is to get the misé en place ready, and premix the spices in a bowl or two. Prep doesn't look too bad.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @AN unsalted butter can sub for ghee.  I used fennel seed instead of ground fennel which I didn’t have. It was fine.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was super delicious. But agree with others, the prep time is critical, and took WAY more than 10 minutes. Even having everything prepped before I started cooking, I think the cooking time was at least 30 minutes. Luckily I picked a Friday night, when I generally get home at a reasonable time and can spend more time cooking. On a Monday night, when I workout before I start cooking, I would not be eating this until almost 10 PM.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was absolutely delicious, despite being out of star anise and fennel. Wonderful flavors and just enough spice - not too much for the kiddos. I doubled the onions because it's my favorite element of biryani. Will try again with minced chicken; definitely going on the rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious! We love Indian food but it’s often a project. This recipe comes together really fast once you assemble the ingredients and do the prep, which you definitely should do first. I used ground lamb because I had it and it seemed more authentic (Hindus don’t do beef).  I had leftover cucumber cumin raita from a few nights ago so served with that, and sliced cucumbers and daikon radishes. Garnished with the cilantro and pomegranate seeds. Looked pretty and added a bit of flavor contrast. K

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Always looking for something new to do with ground chuck (=hamburger), so gave this a try tonight. 

Mise en place is your game plan. Measure out your spices early in the day and prep your onion and garlic and you’ll be ready to roll at mealtime.

Used regular long-grain rice. After cooking the rice for 10 minutes per the recipe, I turned off the heat and by the time I was ready to combine the meat and rice, the rice was done.

3 T of ghee made this a very rich recipe. Next time I’ll use vegetable oil to sauté the onions ( which I’ll double ) and use 1 T ghee to bloom the spices. This is not a wildly spicy dish; next time I’ll kick it up a notch (more chile? More cumin?) to suit our tastes. I thought of adding chile crisp at the table.
Next time I’ll add peas and raisins, per other commenters.

Used ground chuck (85/15) and next time will drain some of the fat.

Some may think this needs more salt. I served it with lime wedges, which were much appreciated. Suggest you add a salad; some contrasting crunch would go well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: What’s the point of the milk drizzled on top of the beef rice mixture?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Sumac
It adds moisture to the partially cooked rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Assuming Green Cardamom not black

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Bibendum I used black cardamom pods based on some research I did. Correct me if I am wrong. The recipe could and should be more specific here.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Bibendum yes, these are green cardamom pods.  Black cardamom is typically not referred to as “pods”.  Also, 1-2 black cardamom pods would be sufficient for a recipe with these portions.  5 green cardamom pods are prefect here!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Used regular long grain rice and vegetable oil instead of ghee.  A tsp of ground ginger was substituted for fresh.  My cooking times were longer and a non-stick pan isn’t necessary for the onion/burger preparation.  Served with peas and naan.  Very good!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Pretty easy if you have all the spices on hand, but left me wanting a better biryani. Flavor combos are good (yes, green cardamom). If I made this again I'd use less beef, and double the chili powder (like Deggi Mirch). In the end the Dishoom Chicken Berry Biryani is about as much work and is much better.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: 1/4 tsp of anything is not enough. Add more.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
There's not much to say in response except, No, 1/4 tsp is often the right amount of a spice.

That's why they make 1/4 tsp measuring spoons....

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Made this recipee yesterday.

Using any more than the 1/4 tsp of fennel would have completely overpowered the other spices in the dish.

The amounts given in this recipe work very well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: From an Indian food snob, this is phenomenal. I was looking for something to do with a leftover pound of hamburger, I keep a lot of spices, and I had no idea such a treat was possible! It was delicious. I might consider toasting and grinding the spices next time, maybe crumbling some toasted saffron into the milk, but it certainly worked as written!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The chicken stock to rice ratio seems off. The grains won’t elongate. I’d think at least another half cup

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made with dark meat turkey, delicious. Next will try browned crumbled tofu.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Alyse I had the same problem. I’m going to have to recook the leftovers with more liquid and hope it doesn’t destroy the dish. Next time I’ll cook the rice first until done and just mix in the meat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Anyone tried this with super firm tofu or chickpeas?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Seemed like too much rice to meat mixture, next time I will half the rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is one the best recipes you guys have ever poster. My family and I devoured it and were so sad when it was over. Very easy to make but the result is impressive and absolutely delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I really liked this dish. My husband thought it was good but not on the ‘great’ list. Will pack the leftovers up and have it ready to take to work this week.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely LOVED this! I know the ingredient list is lengthy, but we happened to have most of them in our pantry already (minus the star anise). It was still delicious!

author: Kay Chun

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: this sounds interesting, but I find myself 5curious why and not use ground lamb if you’re going to “cheat” on a lamb dish?  But maybe it’s just cause ground beef is so readily available.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I too have Indian husband so ingredients at hand. He usually works all day to make his own from scratch and it’s delicious. His Birthdays coming up so maybe I’ll give this a try! I’m not an all day kind of girl, so I’ll be curious and hopeful about the outcome.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: We found this to be a very flavorful and delicious meal.  I followed the recipe as written but a few notes for next time.  I will par cook the rice a little less before adding the meat.  Also, I used ground turkey and I found that the meat seemed to dissolve into the rice.  Next time I will use either cubed chicken or beef.  Otherwise a great recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Does ground fennel mean ground fennel seeds? Or is there also ground fennel out there?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Dawn I grind my own fennel or just put it in whole.  Amazon sells inexpensive grinders similar to coffee grinders but with two interchangeable, washable grinding cups, one for wet ingredients, the other for dry ingredients.  They’re great for grinding spice mixtures, ginger garlic paste, and individual spices.  I’ve never seen ground fennel in stores.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: added equal parts mint & cilantro

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Can someone advise? It did not take 10 minutes for the rice to cook and I am totally stumped on what I did wrong!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The 1/4 tsp of cumin seed that go into the cumin raita should be toasted.  Preferably toasted and coarsely ground.  Raw cumin in yogurt is no bueno!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious - entire family gobbled it up and labeled it a "keeper". The only change I'll make next time is to double it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Bren I'll try grated super-firm tofu next time so our vegetarians can enjoy this too. See the NYT recipe for "I Can't Believe It's Not Chicken" https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1027269-i-cant-believe-its-not-chicken-super-savory-grated-tofu?smid=ck-recipe-android-share for technique. Terrific way to use tofu in a variety of dishes!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The payoff on your effort here is astronomically high. Though the ingredients list is long, the miseing is quick, straightforward, and mostly leaning on dry, shelf-stable ingredients, which means the shopping list will be short next time. The cooking is delightful, fun, and foolproof. And oh, Lord, is it tasty. My wife is away for work, and normally I wouldn't make something this elaborate for myself, but I will. My only fear is nothing will stop me from having these "4 servings" in a sitting.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Can I substitute ground cardamon for the pods?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Yes, 1/2 tsp ground cardamom is equivalent to 5 pods.  It worked for me!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was tasty, but the portions were very large and I think 1 rather than 1-1/2 cups of rice would be enough.  I got 5 generous meals out of it.   It tasted great on reheating.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Great aroma, not enough flavor. If I cook this again, I'll use half as much rice and more chili.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The rice came out great. Used 1 cup rice with 1.5 cups water, and 1/2 vegetable broth cube. I think the beef part could use more spices and mine needed more salt. Forgot about the cilantro and adding peas and raisons. Was also going to add some fenugreek at the end which I forgot to try. Overall, has potential and was not bad. Perhaps my garam masala is not as fresh as it should be.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Zozoxoe

It’s funny how the ingredients list calls for low sodium broth, and yet in the directions suggests adding salt four different times.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I'd like to try this, but cardamon pods aren't something I keep on hand.  I know people will say no way! or it won't be as good, but...would cardamom powder be a reasonable substitute?  If so, how much powder per pod?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Loisp, I substitute with 1 cardamom pod= 1/4 tsp ground

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Try buying decorticated cardamom (it’s the interior of the green cardamom pod) and grind that up when needed in a recipe.  I have a FinaMill grinder on my counter with separate grinder pods of salt, whole black pepper, and the decorticated cardamom (which I also grind into my cappuccino every day and into hot tea).

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
My family (both Indian and non-Indian) loved this! I used brown Basmati rice which I forgot to rinse. Next time I will add chopped raw cashews, and possibly sultanas, and serve on a platter edged with sliced hard boiled eggs. Delicious!!!
@Gina, sometimes Indian spice lists seem to go on forever! Assemble the spice containers first, then use small bowls to make the mixes that will be added together throughout the recipe. I didn't have Garam Masala, but Google suggested a recipe!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I often substitute game meat for beef. It’s leaner and healthier, I believe. Either elk or deer

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was heart-warming though a bit complicated.   It makes four generous servings, at least. Though I was skeptical the rice would get cooked as directed, the rice came out perfectly.  If I made it again would double (at least) the spices, except for the chile (though our spices may be a bit old).

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! My first time cooking Indian food (I know, right?) - and this was the perfect introduction. Now that i have all the spices I'm ready to move on to the next challenge. Yummy, warm spices - a great one-dish winter meal. I added frozen peas so that we could get our veggies and still have it in one pot.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: My teens did not really care for this, and if Indian food isn't your thing, this probably won't be a hit for you either. However, I thought it was amazing. It came together easily, and I was thrilled to be able to use many of the exotic (to me) spices that had been languishing in my pantry. I couldn't find ground fennel, though, so I crushed fennel seeds with a mortar and pestle instead.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Nice taste, but not so beautiful to look at. Our children used to call such plates "pirate's food", everything is mixed together...

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Madeleine that is a Biryani. I have never had one that made a beautiful plate but boy is it tasty.

recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Russell Yes, the cow is sacred for Hindus, but other religious groups do eat beef. In India Hindus would make it with lamb.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: My favorite Indian restaurant serves beef. I don't think generalities apply, in any case.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Russell my in-laws are Muslim Indian and I’ve learned several beef recipes from my mother-in-law. The entire country isn’t Hindu.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Lots of ingredients but quite straightforward to make. Served with sliced radicchio topped with the yogurt dressing. Will definitely make again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Increased the rice (and liquid) for hungry teens and used less cayenne but otherwise made as it. Topped with Trader Joe's fried shallots and served with a veggie side dish. Everyone enjoyed it and agreed we could make it again. Next time would add a little more heat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @LLinDallasTry finely chopped green chilies in the onion mix

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone had good results subbing dried spices for the cardamom pods and cinnamon sticks?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: If you have a well-stocked spice pantry, this comes together quickly and easily. We are huge fans of Indian cuisine, so this was a perfect weeknight meal that came together quickly with. Will be added to our regular rotation. Going to substitute ground lamb next time. Very delicious!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
added carrots after the onion was cooked and then a cup of frozen peas to the meat before adding to the rice. 

added cucumber and lemon juice to the raita.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Very tasty.  Something I will do next time - put the spices in a cheesecloth bag (or spice bags which I get on amazon) so you're not picking cardamom pods or cloves from your forkfuls.

Next time I will also start the beef sautee at the same time I start the rice, as for me the rice finished first.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I had all the ingredients and spices on hand other than the ground beef and cinnamon sticks (discovered today TJoe's doesnt have them!) so I used roasted cinnamon powder and added that with the other dry spices.  Doubled the recipe for a large group.  It was delicious and took less than an hour from start to finish, including any prep.  Not sure it is at all traditional but I'd certainly make this again

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Sorry, this was more Betty Crocker than Madhur Jaffrey. Lacked flavor and that Indian punch! Won’t repeat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: It was a hit with 3 out of 4 of us.  I thought it was delicious and my son and wife said to make it again.  My daughter, who loves rice and enjoys Thai and Indian food, didn't care for it.  Yes, there is a long list of spices so I measured them out in advance and put them in separate prep bowls with the others that were added at the same time.  Still took me longer than 20 minutes, mostly because the onion and beef took more time because our cooktop has just one large burner.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made this with ground chicken and added some greens at the end (chard). The rice took me about 10 minutes longer to cook all the way through and I had to add more water to it. Otherwise a delicious, flavorful, and simple recipe!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Very happy with this dish!  For everyone saying the ingredient list is too long, I found it really helpful to prep the two sets of spices earlier in the day, along with slicing the onion.  Then the actual cooking came together really easily and quickly.

The recipe didn’t specify black or green cardamom pods.  I used black but I’m sure either would be fine.  The aromas in the house right now are amazing!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe - came together quickly and my husband got 3 servings! My 2 year old enjoyed it, too. As a plus - this is a great excuse to visit my local Indian market for some good spices. Will definitely come back to this one!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I cooked this. I had no issues with the ingredients as I have all the Indian spices required in daily cooking. However, in the end it was just kheema mixed with rice for me.  Nothing special. It tasted fine.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I learned of this recipe from @rachels_free_thyme and this dish was amazing! It’s worth the time to get the spices - it was extremely flavorful & very simple. Adding this to my recipe rotation!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Very easy (even with lots of spices) and definitely a comfort food.  Indian-style hamburger helper if you will.  Didn’t have plain yogurt so subbed out with sour cream and it worked beautifully!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was extremely good, and extremely easy! A really big dividend on the time invested. This recipe will go into regular rotation. I had to add a bit more water to the rice, as my large dutch oven held too much heat after the parboil.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: We enjoyed it with ground lamb swapped in for beef.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Am I missing something? I can't seem to find the instruction about what to do with the broth and when/where to add the rice?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Broth and rice are used in Step 1. I missed the rice at first glance, too.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe! I had the Curry Spice Blend from Auria's Malaysian Kitchen on hand and used it as a shortcut. Didn't disappoint. Local to Brooklyn, too!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Categrace I see no reason why you shouldn't,  and as a lamb lover, that'd be my choice, too!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Having cooked beef Biryani many times (a couple hours at least), this recipe was excellent. Very flavorful, easy, can make it non-spicy or add more spice or a chutney on the side.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: We enjoyed this but I def did adapt it baed on other folks’ comments. Was excited to use some ground lamb I had in the freezer, that is for sure the way to go. Added some leftover hash brown potatoes I needed t and some frozen peas I was glad to use up. I doubled the hot curry power (I get mine from Kalustyan’s) and slightly increased the garam masala, next time I will dial it up even more. Was grateful to read the other notes re how long the rice actually needed.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Really good, thought it would be a bit dry, but it was lovely. Added a small chilli for a little more kick, and also used minced lamb, as a couple of people had suggested, that worked really well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Really good, thought it would be a bit dry, but it was lovely. Added a handful of peas, and a small chilli for a little more kick, and also used minced lamb, as a couple of people had suggested, really yummy, family enjoyed it, definitely worth doing again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: While I knew my husband probably wouldn’t like this, I made it anyway. He deemed it ‘okay,’ but I loved it! I’d like to try it with chicken! 5 stars all the way!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I had all the spices and aromatics so I made this last night.  It was alright, nothing spectacular. You would think the flavors and aroma could be overwhelming, but it was actually underwhelming.  This is not a recipe I'll keep iny rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
I used cayenne pepper in place of the Kashmiri chile powder and accidentally put in the 1/2tsp instead of the 1/4 tsp for the cayenne and man is this spicy hot.
However, I think the ratio of rice in the recipe is just a little to much but I like more substance to my meal than primarily rice. I may try it again but bump up the meat and onion mix or just cut back the rice by 1/4 cup.
The flavor is good minus the bit too much spice heat due to my mistake

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed, and it was really delicious with wonderfully complex just right flavors. Next time I might add a bit more tomato paste to make it saucer and more colorful. Mine was brown and the photo looks more orange. Super satisfying!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Flavor was good, but I would recommend against the cardamom pods and whole cloves. They are very hard to spot/remove and biting into one as you eat is a fairly unpleasant experience. I understand using them makes it a more authentic experience, but seeing as how this is already a "cheater version", I would think using ground seasoning instead would be both more convenient and more pleasant.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: You can double up the whole spices and put them in a cheese cloth bag - makes them easy to find and subsequently remove :)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Seth. Tiesta Tea Filter bags are my go-to for whole spices, as well as loose leaf tea.  I’ve found them at the supermarket and also Amazon. Easy to remove once cooking is completed.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I thought cows were sacred? I’ve never in my life seen beef on an Indian restaurant menu, but ok, I’ll try it lol.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made with ground lamb. Loved the spices and only had to sub one. However, next time I’ll completely cook the meat mixture separately from the rice. Don’t see any significant benefit since the meat juices can be mingled with the fully cooked rice before serving. Just my preference.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was incredibly delicious! I substituted the beef with 96 percent beef and used European low-fat yogurt. It turned out to be a super high-protein meal!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made this for the family tonight, exactly as the recipe is written. Everyone loved it, even the 9 and 7yr old, and we all had 2nds and 3rds. Really delicious especially with the yogurt on top. All the spices matter!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I prepped most of this the night before while cleaning up dinner dishes. It came together in a flash the next day. I use this prep one day cook the next technique a lot. The amount of time overall is about the same but dinner is on the table at least 45 minutes earlier when I'm not starting from scratch after I get home from work.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone tried this using brown rice? Trying to use some up!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Used shallots instead of onion, but otherwise followed the recipe. It was delicious! Will definitely go in the rotation!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Not sure what else I can say!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. I added a wee bit of asafoetida to the onion sauté and some saffron to the milk. But it legit took 2 hours. I wish NYT Cooking would be more realistic.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: A very successful easy recipe. I forfeited some authenticity by not layering the beef and the rice. I found it more convenient to fully cook the rice and its spices ahead, then add it to the cooked beef. I don't I think sacrificed taste, though. This was delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Really tasty after some adjustments. Made as is, I think the rice to meat ratio is off and it's under seasoned. I reduced the rice to 1 c. and I almost doubled the spices.  I also added frozen peas for the taste and fiber, big fan of all in one meals! Lastly, I didn't make the raita the second time I made the dish. It was fine, but a big blob of yogurt was just as good.  With the tweaks, it's something we'll put on our monthly rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Oh also, a few mint leaves added in with the cilantro is traditional, and a nice touch.  Especially if instead of ground beef you use ground lamb (which was my first thought when reading the recipe.)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Made this today but didn't have Basmati rice and reading other's comments that Basmati is needed, I cooked Jasmine rice in my rice cooker using the spices called for,  , stopping before fully cooked. The spices came to the top after cooking so were easy to remove. 
The rest of the recipe I followed as written. I had some goat burger left in my freezer, probably by one of my son's friends who has left other meats in the past! (Great kid). The goat burger had a lot of fat in it but I sopped it up with paper towels and that worked great.  I pushed the meat to one side in the pan, filled the rest of the pan with the rice and scooped the meat over it and spread both out evenly, then cooked a bit more to finish the rice and let the flavors marry.  I like crispy rice on the bottom of these type of dishes so let it cook a bit longer for some crunch.  Husband loved it. I liked it too. It made several servings. After we had our share there about 4 more servings . Lunch for the week!  

A now that I have made this once and understand the steps needed, I think I can pull it together more quickly but this took me 2 hours including prep-- and hunting down spices in my overflowing spice cabinet.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The timing on the recipe is a mess! Start the onions before the rice, and everything takes longer to cook than stated.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious, easier, & faster. My family all asked me to save this recipe- which is a huge win!

I doubled the rice and spices to stretch the meal further. I used grass fed ground beef & homemade chicken stock for the rice. After reading the comments I put raisins on the side as a topping option for my family - they all added them so next time I will place them between the rice & beef. 
I am glad I started the onions first as I like them slightly caramelized. 

I was frustrated at myself that I didn’t plan for a vegg with dinner so we had fresh baby spinach on the side- thankfully a little bit of the cumin sauce made a nice dressing. For under $15 for an organic meal for 4 (including 2 teenagers) this is a win!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was so good! I 1.5x the recipe but used 2 lbs. of beef and it still felt like too much rice to beef for our protein lovers in the family.  The flavor; however, was outstanding!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: We loved it but next time I'll used 1.5 pounds of beef.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Great base recipe. When I make it again I’d use ginger garlic paste to save chopping time. 
I’d also double the spices, add cucumber to the Raita, and a generous squeeze of lime to the final dish. I also added hing because it was in my pantry, but not worth seeking out if you don’t already have it. Same deal with saffron. Great (and traditional) addition, but not necessary. 
Overall, a great place to start, but as a certified Indian food lover, I think amping up the spices a bit goes a long way.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Am I missing how to cook the mixture for the final 8 min? On a stovetop on low? In the oven (if so, at what temp)?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: What kind of beef?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: 1pound ground beef (preferably 20 percent fat).  Duh!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Substitute for whole milk to avoid lactose issues?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I would sub with a little extra broth or water - or if you want the creaminess and nuts aren't an issue, coconut milk or cashew cream.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
I want to make this for a group. Can I double or triple it? 
Would you consider adding that advice in more of your recipes? 
Many of them could be used at a dinner party not just a family of four.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: You don't drain the fat from the beef?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Fat = flavor. You could use 85%, 90%, or 93% ground beef if fat content is a concern.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
@Emmy
I used a very lean ground beef and it was a bit dry. Recipe recommends 80% which is likely better.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Any suggestions for substitutions for those of us who cannot eat mammal meat? Would ground chicken or turkey provide enough depth of flavor?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I substitute ground turkey (93%) often in similar Indian dishes that list beef, pork, or lamb and find the result very satisfying and more healthy.  Give it a try.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I can’t eat tomato or tomato paste. Would omitting it compromise the end flavours?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Marney O Yes, but if you can eat red bell peppers, then a purée from roasted red bells would be a good substitute.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I never put it in biryani when I make it in the pressure cooker, I think you'll still have a delicious meal.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Does the dutch oven stay on low heat on the hob after adding the beef or does it get moved to the oven at any point? I’m a bit afraid of the meat not being done or the rice burning

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Do you think this would work with leftover braised beef?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: No, Beth, but thanks for asking

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Why not give it a try?  I often recycle leftover roast into curries and chilis and, since this recipe has so many lovely spices, I feel sure that the result will still be worth the effort.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I gather that step 6 is the cumin raita, even though it only uses 1/4 teaspoon of cumin?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Would ground lamb work?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Yes, lamb will work in place of beef.  Lamb is more fatty than most ground beef so you will definitely want to drain the fat after browning the meat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: If you add ground lamb it's going to be very, very oily.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
From my teen: “What the heck??? This was so good. You have to make it again.” 

Stayed loyal to the recipe except for the raita: added mint and shredded some cucumber into to yogurt instead of adding water.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Quick, easy and delicious weeknight meal. I only had to buy the ground beef, everything else had on hand. Served with a green salad and naan. Excellent. Will definitely put it in the rotation. Thank you!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: But it's almost all spices! Not really so many ingredients, mostly beef, rice, and yogurt. @Gina

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: But, Judith, as others have pointed out, most of the ingredients are just spices.@judith

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Good. I doubled the beef and trebled the spices. I also layered the rice so the beef was sandwiched in between and added a whole onion sliced and fried on top. Would be great with lamb and also some chiles in there.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: It's just the two of us. Can we freeze half for another quick dinner?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Mmmmm… Sacred Cow Biryani!  Very traditional and authentic!  Or possibly an abomination?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Not all hold the cow sacred. Many see it as a prime source of meat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
So delicious!  Tons of flavor.  Relatively quick and easy once you have your spices organized.  
One question - ingredients list cumin seed, recipe refers to cumin.  I was tempted to use ground cumin for the raita as the seed didn't give a ton of flavor but wanted to stick to the recipe as written the first time around.  Has anyone used ground instead of seed?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @B. I used ground and thought it worked great

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @B. I’m not an expert but the raw seed in the raita seems odd to me. I love it with ground cumin.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious, but I had trouble managing it… even with doing almost all the prep up front, I was still back and forth to drawers and fridge and cabinet frantically.
I made the NYT lemon kale salad as a side, and my husband just dumped the 2 dishes into one bowl and said it was better that way.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made this for dinner tonight—upped the spices in general but not a whole lot, and used chopped boneless chicken thighs in place of ground meat bc I just don’t love ground meats. Didn’t have enough cilantro, so augmented it with basil. Minor tweaks, I think. This one is definitely a keeper!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: To me this is an Indian spiced version of hamburger helper. If I make it again I would increase the chili powder.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Why does Step 5 say to layer the beef over the rice....and Step 7 says to fluff the rice and remove the visible spices?  And why has no one else asked this question?  I mean, I have made Biryani before and can figure out what to do, but shouldn't recipes make sense?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Step 5 doesn't contradict Step 7. You cook the meat on top of the rice in the oven, and then before serving, fluff everything and remove the whole spices.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Grind your cardamom pods before putting in the dish.  I couldn’t find them all and it is unpleasant to bite into a pod

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Good! use actual basmati next time

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Question. I have a neurospicy son with texture issues. Anyone ever try this with stew beef chuck?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Your recipe doesn't say what to do with the ½ cup yogurt, cumin, water, salt & pepper mixture.  Is it a topping?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Step 6: 
Meanwhile in a small bowl, combine the remaining ½ cup yogurt, ¼ teaspoon cumin and 2 tablespoons of water and season with salt and pepper. Mix well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Yes. It's a topping. You serve it on the side and drizzle on top before eating.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: What’s a good cut of beef to swap in for ground beef?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I don't use ghee often enough to keep it in stock. Would it be very dreadful to substitute vegetable oil of some kind?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @JennyD use unsalted butter if not ghee

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Anyone ever add squash of sweet potatoes to this?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is very good and as advertised — a simple weeknight meal.  One minor comment that I haven’t seen previously — the amount of stock (2cups) is woefully inadequate for 1-1/2 cups of rice.  I doubled the amount of stock and it came out perfect.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made largely as directed.  The only minor changes.  First, 2 cups of broth wasn't enough for the rice so I added another 1/2 - 3/4 cup and that allowed the rice to fully cook.  Of course, different rices absorb differently so you'll have to watch and make adjustments as needed.  Second, I didn't have any cilantro so couldn't include it.  Last, I added frozen peas to cook for the last minute and they made a nice addition. The spice blend and levels were spot on.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I made this dish exactly as written and my husband and two adult sons loved it to the point I had no leftovers.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
In spite of the number of ingredients this is easy, and quick if you pull the spices together before hand. 
It was utterly delicious, but my rice did not cook fully. I’m hoping the leftovers will be moister, as the rice should absorb more moisture while stored in the fridge overnight. I followed the recipe very carefully, so the undercooked rice surprised me. Anyone else? Any suggestions?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Katherine Davies mine too. I think I maybe turned it down too low? Need time I will cover it, as I suspected I should have :/

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Katherine Davies i just reread the recipe and realized they said to cover the rice earlier on so i didn’t cover it while it cooked. I generally wait for rice to boil then cover… the recipe states to cover early- but how do you know it’s boiling? lol

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I've made 'proper' breyani many times, so was sceptic about the shortcuts in this recipe. But it really did taste like 'proper' breyani! (Minus the different textures of the traditional.) I doubled the recipe and left it to finish for much longer than the suggested 8 minutes - was delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Could you not just do the first step in a rice cooker, then top it with the beef mixture?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I have made this as written, and was thinking the same thing!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight.  I didn't have basmati rice, so used jasmine... probably not the best choice, it was mushy.  I think I'd like to try it using 100% wild rice?  I expected the dish to be more of a flavor bomb than it was, given all the spices used.  Except for the type of rice, I followed the recipe to the "T".  Husband loved it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The timing seems off.  My rice never got fully cooked.  Even after 30min of cooking time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is a delicious dish, but the cumin was too pronounced. Easy fix: For the raita, brighten it by omitting cumin and subbing in peeled, deseeded, and diced cucumber; finish lemon zest.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: How much of each spice would you use if using powdered spices instead of cinnamon sticks, whole cloves, etc?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Very easy to make and delicious!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was very good! As others have suggested I’ll steep a few Saffron threads in the milk and swap the ground beef for ground lamb to amp up the flavors even more. This was a total winner!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: The most disappointing 5-star recipe I have ever tried on NYT cooking - and I have cooked a lot of them!  I even increased the garlic, ginger and Kashmiri chili and it was still just blah!  Not bad, just blah!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Twice the broth, more salt, in a 350 degree oven for 20 minutes. Good with pork too. Used fennel seeds instead of ground fennel. Added lemon juice to the raita for more tang

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! Didn't have Greek yogurt, so used European style and it was fine - I just didn't need to add the water to the half used for serving. Would make double the amount of seasoned yogurt for serving next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Quick, easy and tasty. Made as written but would add a little more heat next time. Perhaps a serrano or two in the rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Dish was excellent and added yogurt at the end gave it  a really nice taste. Question: recipe says add 3 star anise and then to be removed later on.  Recipe doesn’t  explain how to remove them. They are all mixed in with the rice and meat.  This info seems to be missing.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: @Cathy whole star anise are very large. Just pick them out with your hand or tongs.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I would imagine one could easily use grated forms of the whole spices called for in this recipe which might make the spice flavors stronger.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
Love this!

I doubled recipe and used one pound of lamb and 1 pound of beef. I also added a green pepper. Easy. Scrumptious for the whole family!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Just great. Fast easy, delicious, it’s a keeper!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is a comforting and yummy dish! I was missing the star anise and cardamom, so I upped the garam masala a titch. Two out of three kids gobbled this up and said they wanted leftovers for tomorrow’s dinner. The third child is fickle and will probably love it tomorrow. This would go well with roasted cauliflower :)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious. Added some cilantro and cucumber to the raita and drizzled a little lemon on the rice before eating.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I made only two small changes,  using regular yogurt as that is what I had (added a bit more to the meat and didn't add water to the Raita), and half the cayenne as I was worried it would be to hot (but it wasn't hot at all and I think the whole amount would have been fine).  It was delicious and I think it might hold the record for the number of times my husband said 'This is really good!' during one meal.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I made this ‘as is’ but with ground chicken. Next time, I’ll reduce the amount of rice or increase the amount of chicken.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This makes a lot of food, and it was tasty. I felt that it needed more of everything in terms of seasoning to taste like restaurant biryani. 1/4 tsp of anything is practically not there! The 1/4 tsp of cayenne possibly being too spicy was especially questionable.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as directed but with 2X the quantity of spices.  Very tasty!  Took about 45 minutes in total, what with assembling and measuring out all the spices.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: So delicious!!! And so easy if you mis en place the spices first! I used brown basmati and the cook time was obviously much longer (40 minutes before putting beef on top). It’s so good!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: If you wanted to add another layer of flavor and "authenticity" (loaded word), add a little rose water to the milk before drizzling it over the rice.  Barely a teaspoon, I'd say.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Nice as written. Our package of ground beef was a little over a pound and used it all. Perhaps that threw off the beef-to-rice ratio, but we didn't mind. Star anise was pretty powerful so might scale that back by half and increase the cumin seeds next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
This was incredibly good!! I don’t eat beef very often - but this will definitely be a recipe I come back to. 
I didn’t add the chili pepper (didn’t want spicy) or the fennel (don’t like it). Used very lean ground beef, fat free yogurt and 2% milk. Came out great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and comes together really easily as written. I decided to add steamed cauliflower for some veggies. Without that, there may have been too much rice. I would add a bit more beef next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Will make again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Do you take the cinnamon stick, cardamom pods, whole cloves out after you cook the rice or do they dissolve when cooking?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Very good. I would suggest start the onion-spice-beef part first (at least get the onions started, and everything else prepped), then get the rice going. Use less ghee too.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: As long as you have the spices, it really is quite simple. I recommend a wee bit less rice and adding currants.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Lol, “ready in 30 minutes.“ It would take me that long just to gather up all of the spices. Looking forward to giving it a try, but let’s be real.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Mine came out sort of greasy and gloppy. could have sworn I followed the recipe exactly so not sure what went wrong. Nice flavor, the texture was just unpleasant.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Easy, quick, and delicious!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: this was so easy but so satisfying. we all loved it - even the kids!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I had some lamb thawed, and was looking for a recipe, and found this. (I've been making a lot of Turkish keftes lately but I felt having something with more of an Indian flavour). I didn't have garam masala at hand so I used Floyd Cardoz Green Tikka Masala, and ground lamb instead of beef. I added a small amount of Chaat Masala as well (about 1/2 teaspoon). And I didn't have any ginger or anise so I used a store brand Pho broth to cook the rice that has both. It turned out very well!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I guess that’s Phoushion!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Very good and kicked up the spice w hot chili oil topping along w the cool yogurt.  Great on a cold winter night.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: If this is a shortcut version, I cringe at the idea of the ‘regular’ version!  So many ingredients, so many steps!  It was however, FANTASTIC!  The flavors are amazing! I followed the recipe exactly except I used 1t. ground cardamom in lieu of pods, and I doubled all spices, as a previous commenter suggested.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: What could I substitute for tomato paste in this recipe? We have a family member with tomato allergy.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Maybe not something you have on hand, but I didn't have any tomato paste, but I had some red curry paste that I used instead, and it turned out great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent! Used brown rice and precooked for 30 minutes. Used venison instead of beef. Used soy milk and fat free Greek yogurt. Also used olive oil instead of butter. Even with these subs it was so delicious and easy.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Can you make in advance?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I don't see why not. There's jyst the two of us so we ate it over several days. Add a little moisture when re-heating it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious and so easy! Great spice mix.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is a wonderful recipe but one thing confused me. It says in Step One to cook the rice for 10 minutes. The next instruction re the rice does not appear until Step 5 to cook the rice along with the beef mixture for 8 minutes. That is not nearly enough time to cook the rice. My rice is not close to ready after an additional 12 minutes. I'm concerned the additional rice cooking time will result in tough beef. Did I miss something here?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Susan, you did not. My rice was nowhere near ready after such a short cooking time. I removed the beef mixture from the heat until the rice was almost done. I cooked the beef with the rice for only about 5-6 minutes until everything was ready.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: This is the same Susan who just wrote about rice cooking time. I just realized the problem may be that I used brown basmati rice that requires a longer cooking time that white rice. I assume I should have cooked the rice much longer in Step One.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight, but used Impossible Ground "Beef" and plant based dairy to make this vegan. And it was delicious! Five stars

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Just incredible - yielded the best dinner my partner and I have made together.  We added a generous spritz of lemon juice to the raita to add a little acid.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: So good!! I did what others suggested and doubled the spices for the beef and it was delicious. The hubby and picky boys gobbled it all up in record time. I just mixed the rice with the beef at the end, rather than layering the beef over the rice and it still came out great. Definitely don’t skip the raita topping, so easy to whip up and adds another dimension of flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty and easy recipe. I added a frozen bag of peas and carrots to get our veggies in. Big hit with the fam.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: Rice dishes like this never seem to turn out for me in a normal pot. I think it’s a “me problem,” though. After I tried this as written and ended up with undercooked rice, I tried again in my rice cooker with great results. Rice, whole spices, broth and milk in the pot, the onion and beef mixture cooked as written and then scraped on top, then whole thing cooked with the “white rice” setting on my zojirushi. Same measurements for everything. Turned out perfect!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody:
I used brown basmati rice, and as Susan noted below, my rice was not remotely ready after a mere 18 minutes. I cooked the rice longer while the beef mixture rested off-heat. When the rice was almost ready, I added the milk & then the beef mixture for another 5-6 minutes. 

Whole family loved it - the teenaged nephew ate two servings.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Ground Beef Biryani With Cumin Raita Recipe
commentBody: I probably said this already, but this is so amazingly delicious and easy.  I think I could eat this every week.  I made no changes, except I used ground cardamom because that’s all I had.  Just so satisfying.  A party in your mouth!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: Larb is really Lao by origin. It’s their national dish. A lot of great Lao food gets credited as Thai

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: @kim n Thanks for the educational tip! Credit where credit is due!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’m a vegetarian and I used coconut aminos for fish sauce and gardein fake beef bits for the meat. I think this recipe needs that beefy flavor compared to tofu to be more authentic to the Laotian dish. I had no troubles with a 1:1 sub and will be making this again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: Used ground chicken instead of beef, and added chopped scallions and mint to the cilantro. Turned out great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight—increased the garlic, reduced the lettuce a bit, and added mint and sliced raw shallots to the salad. Delicious! Will make again

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: There are shared culinary traditions in many Asian countries, especiallythose that have common borders. Laab features more in northern Thailand, which shares a border with Laos. There are differences in their versions. Similarly, some southern Thai dishes have common roots with Malaysian cuisine. The history behind countries also has a strong influence on their culinary traditions.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
commentBody: Like the recipe but the proportions seemed off when I tried it. Biggest problem: way too much lettuce (8 cups? Seems...wrong? I mean look at the photo here, there's just not that much lettuce.). and I made a lot more sauce (mostly adding more lime juice and didn't regret it, and doubled the garlic. Adding a bit of mint was a good move also. But otherwise -- yeah great flavors will make again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Laab Noodle Salad Recipe
