recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This is good as written but.... it's fairly bland. Second time around I followed the recipe but added  S&B curry powder, bird peppers, fish sauce, Golden Mountain and more lime juice. I also used ground pork. This recipe is a good skeleton but it just needed some oomph.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for dinner tonight, had it on the table in less than hour.  I always read the comments for helpful advice and made the following adjustments: doubled the garlic, ginger, sweet potato and spinach, used a full can of coconut milk.  Diced and roasted the sweet potatoes in 425 degree oven for 30 minutes.  Served this over jasmine rice with generous dose of hot sauce.  It was delicious and perfect for a cold winter evening!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this and loved it with no adjustments to the recipe.  I think it would work well as a vegetarian recipe by eliminating the meat.  Serve over rice.  I like another posters idea of using shrimp!  I’ll try that next time.  It’s flavorful and satisfying!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Using readers suggestions I doubled the garlic, ginger, scallions, cilantro, lime, and used a full can of coconut milk. My tweaks were that after sautéing the potatoes for eight minutes I added two bags of frozen spinach and let them cook down so their liquid could help soften the potatoes. Then I removed the spinach and sweet potatoes and proceeded with the recipe as instructed. I added fish sauce and garlic salt to the ground turkey mixture. Delicious recipe and will be on rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Good with chicken breast instead of ground meat, Red pepper flakes and lots of salt helps much as I try to avoid excess salt. Leftover coconut milk can go straight into the rice fat and all. Great.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I agree with those who enjoyed it.  It is a great base, but go with your gut! Add more of the ingredients you like and call it a day.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: It was better the next day.....hubs' comment was, 'nothing to write home about'! I had to add a spoonful of chicken Better Than Bouillon to improve the flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Considering all the interesting ingredients, I expected this have much more robust flavor. Don’t think I’ll make it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I loved this and my teenage daughter who resists sweet potatoes was very enthusiastic! Is it possible to sub in ground turkey? Also, I agree with some of the other comments and would add red pepper flakes next time for a bit more flavor. This is definitely going in the rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This is an easy cook with a delicious and generous yield. The whole family liked it, even the 2yo and pickier 6yo. Didn't deviate from recipe other than to add crushed peanuts as a heavy garnish at the end and that, my friends, was a power move for this recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used most of the flavor-boosting modifications suggested in the comments, and it was very tasty. Microwaved the diced sweet potato before giving it a quick sauté. Would be good garnished with bean sprouts. Keep the sweet potato peels and make quick snap chips with them in oven or air fryer.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  I used lean ground turkey because I did not want to make a grocery store run.  The flavor profile looked good but after reading comments, I doubled scallions, ginger, and garlic.  Again, delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious, quick, and easy!  I used precut butternut squash because I was lazy. :)  Also didn't have any ginger on hand - I added a hint of cinnamon, but I don't think it needed it (nor did it need hot sauce)!  Normally I buy 99% fat free ground chicken, but I went less lean this time and believe the recipe was better for it.  Highly recommend - very flavorful and hearty - will make again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Used a full can of coconut milk and chopped spinach into smaller pieces. I added the lime juice at the end, but felt it needed more once we were eating over the rice. Ended up cutting up a lime to squeeze over individual portions at the table and that was perfect.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: this recipe should sit for about 15 minutes after cooking, it thickens and the flavors become more pronounced.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I love the flavors in this. The sweet potatoes took longer than 8 minutes to soften, and I intermittently added a few tablespoons of water to the pan to help speed up cooking and prevent burning. The second time I made it I amped things up a bit: added extra ginger, garlic, and scallions, plus the rind of one lime to the chicken. And I added fish sauce and chilli flakes with the coconut milk. These additions worked really well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: made as written but added sriracha at end for some kick and omitted cilantro because I have that gene.  It was easy and delicious but felt maybe fish sauce may have helped?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I'm new to NYT cooking so maybe my expectations are lower than others but I made the recipe as written (minus using dark meat) and didn't find it flat at all. I used 1/2tsp salt and 1/4tsp pepper for each of the first two seasoning steps and just a sprinkle for the third. Agree with others that the ginger, garlic, and coconut milk could be scaled up to taste, and I might next time, but it was good as written. The lime really brightens it up. Can stand alone without rice, in my opinion.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night- delish! Followed suggestions to double ginger and garlic. Threw in a tsp green curry paste while frying them. Used a whole can of coconut milk. One thing I hate is the watery residue when wilting spinach, so I doubled the spinach and wilted it with the chicken, as soon as the chicken was no longer pink, and before adding coconut milk. I let all the spinach “water” boil off first. Worked out great! Served with jasmine rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Kid approved! Everyone loved it. I had ground turkey on hand and used that instead. Delicious and flavorful. Will be added to our family favorites.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I've made this a couple times now.  We use spicy mix baby greens instead of baby spinach.  I toss the yams with oil, salt and ground Espelette pepper and roast the yams in the oven.  This adds a little kick to this dish that we thought it needed.  Great dish!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Typical recipe with overwhelming garlic and onion. Other ingredients dampened by their continuous use in all recipes here. Stop their use and the return will be an appreciation of the other tastes returning.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: After reading other comments, I doubled the garlic and ginger and added a heaping tsp of curry powder and 1/4 tsp of chili powder. I still needed hot sauce.  I had leftovers and didn’t want to eat rice again, so warmed them and served with lettuce wraps and hot sauce.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: My picky five year old ate three servings, and Mom and Dad (both foodies) enjoyed it too. If that’s not five stars, I don’t know what is.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I would substitute winter squash for the sweet potato if I needed to.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Even after adding extra garlic and ginger, the BF and I thought this was lacking both flavor and texture. It's ok -- healthy and hearty but also blah and mooshy.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Add a touch of fish sauce when you add the coconut milk.  Thank me later.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Made with ground pork. Would make again. Good and comforting but not exciting per se. Truman called it "thai-inspired hamburger helper". Both added soy sauce or hot sauce.

Next time:
- drain off some fat (if using pork)
- More / larger sweet potato chunks
- Could add more spinach (full 5 oz container)
- add fish sauce?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as written, will be on repeat in our house .

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Like a lot of other people said, if one uses the full 1lb/500g of minced meat, one basically needs to double all the other ingredients. I did and it worked out great. I also added frozen lime leaves, and a teaspoon of palm sugar. I also added carrots and (at the end) fried zucchini. Very good mid week dinner - will be cooking it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Add shiitake mushrooms, fish sauce, 3 limes, light brown sugar, two Birds Eye chili

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I was so excited about this recipe given the high ratings, but it was bland and lacked depth. I considered adding more spices throughout - cumin? Wish I had. Hot sauce at the end is a lazy substitute for depth/spice. Next time, I’ll try that- and go with ground turkey instead of chicken.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I added some rooster sauce for spice and oven roasted the sweet potatoes. Served over brown rice. Super yummy!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was a tasty dish, but as another reviewer stated: there is no way this is prepared in 20 minutes! What we really like about this dish is that it combines interesting ingredients and flavors. I did stray from the recipe to combine sausage and chopped chicken thighs as the meat - otherwise followed the recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Very glad I read comments! Added fish sauce, red pepper flakes, and doubled the garlic and ginger. Super flavorful that way—can imagine the recipe as is might be a little lackluster. Still rated five stars! Will def make again. 

Also, I ended up using the whole coconut milk can. Kale and kale florets instead of spinach because I had them :)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I like it...but it needs a kick...other than hot sauce...maybe use sesame oil?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Can you sub frozen spinach?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Easy and very good. I used ground dark meat turkey, added extra spinach and ginger and garlic, and also added 2 t of Japanese gochujang for some spice and depth of flavor. Excellent!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was quite good & easy. I concur with JW who mentioned increasing the size of the sweet potatoes. I tend to let the main course sit (or simmer) while getting the other bits done, so the sweet potatoes got a bit mushy. I always increase the garlic, and I think I could have done more (at least double), along with more ginger. Diced up a chicken breast as I didn't have anything ground & worked just fine. I increased scallions & spinach-should do so with cilantro. Hot sauce was a good add.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I typically skip garlic and onion as my partner has an allium intolerance. I followed advice of others to increase ginger and acid and also added some curry powder to my ground turkey. Fantastic pay off for a quick simple meal.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Simple and delicious. I wouldn't change a thing.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This wasn’t BAD. But it definitely wasn’t good. It turned out to be very bland, even after doubling the garlic and ginger. We do not stan sweet potato in this household, so used regular potato instead - I always expect the recipe to turn out a little bit worse when there are substitutions. But this was just.. not it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Would it come out ok with light coconut milk?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Added some Thai basil, yellow curry, and some red pepper flakes.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was very good. Super simple but I did add a couple things. Diced red bell pepper, 2 tbls of tamari plus 1 teaspoon of fish sauce and 1 Thai chili. Delicious and easy. Also sub’d turkey for chicken.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I bet you could cut up sweet potato fires for an easier prep.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: My boyfriend and I thought this dish was good but needed more flavor. I added some chili pepper flakes and we couldn’t taste much besides that, I would definitely double the amount of garlic/ginger next time. I could see making this again as an easy meal prep dish though, and call me nuts, but I think it’d be pretty good with a fried egg on top.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Adding this to my rotation!  This dish was so easy to make and so delicious!  I added more lime juice because I love the punch.  Can’t wait to make again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this for dinner tonight and it was scrumptious.  I made a few small modifications like using a large sweet potato and the whole can of coconut milk.  The magic ingredient was a few sprigs of Thai basil, chopped, and added with the cilantro.  Plenty for a second dinner for 2 and maybe a lunch.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was very bland for me. I added a bit of fish sauce and a bit of sugar to make it more tasty otherwise you need to add a lot of salt.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is fantastic! I added fish sauce at the end, with the cilantro and lime. Will definitely be adding to my rotation!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I just made this as is and I’m underwhelmed. No spices? Looks like something I’d see on a weight watchers meal plan. 5 stars…did I miss something??

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent. Made as recipe calls for but added a spoonful of Fly by Jing Zhong Sauce for a tiny little zip.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Nice recipe. I doubled the ginger and added more garlic. A chili pepper would have been nice too. Simple to make.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was a great easy recipe with an enjoyable flavor profile. I do think it could use some more spinach and sweet potato and the base of the dish lacked a little bit of flavor so it definitely needed the hot sauce but with hot sauce it brought it to the next level. I agree with the other comments saying it could use more of the aromatics. Regardless all the ingredients go together really well!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Good as written, great with a tablespoon of fish sauce and an extra lime added to the broth.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
This was just ok. It was really the odd combo of textures that threw me off. And it wasn't enough coconut milk to be like a curry, not dry enough to be like a hash/skillet, so it was just kind of gloopy. Literal slop bowl. 

Unfortunate since it seemed like an easy to digest, easy to cook meal for the week.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Definitely needs more lime juice and…something. It’s just a bit bland. But with tweaking could be great. More ginger? Lemongrass? Something.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this dish - very easy cooking experience. It did feel a tad flavorless compared to other similar dishes I’ve made. I actually added a drizzle of soy sauce which added some needed depth to the flavor. Agree with some of the other comments that suggest upping the aromatics. Overall, hearty, healthy, and delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I used chicken thighs cut to bite-sized pieces so we could eat with chop sticks and give more bite to the dish. Thinned with some leftover chicken broth I had on hand.  Doubled the spinach per other comments —good move! Served over basmati rice. Loved it!! Fun to make too!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written except I used two 13 ounce microwaveable butternut squash packages just to make it easier. I cooked them in the microwave and then put them in the frying pan with the hot oil. Otherwise, no changes and it was delicious over white rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I incorporated many of other readers' comments, and this was a major winner! It was a comfort food entree with very  pleasing flavor, and is a recipe that I will make over and over and would serve to anyone. As others, I doubled the garlic, ginger, and used the whole can of coconut milk. I also added a chopped jalapeño with the garlic; cubed two medium sweet potatoes; probably doubled the lime and inserted the zest as well; and tossed in a lot of spinach. I also added a shake of garam masala; 2 shakes would be fine. Ample scallion, cilantro toppings & a splash of lime juice gave a nice finish. Delish!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made the dish per the recipe and I think it has potential. I think it needs more of a number of ingredients: double the spinach and scallions, increase the garlic and ginger. I think the lime juice needs to be increased but recommend tasting and adding more according to your preference.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: The questions about pans - I just used a seasoned wok.  Added couple T of water about halfway through potato cook.  Used full can of coconut milk and kept heat on to wilt spinach.  Needs more lime plus fish sauce instead of more onion, etc.  Adding heat - Thai chilies or Seranos would be good.  Agree this is a good recipe template from which to work.  Will be working variations.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Really enjoyed this dish.  My family, with varying palates, made these amendments:

1. Add curry powder
2. Add chili crisp
3. Add ‘This Little Goat Went to India’
4. None

Everyone enjoyed!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: 4 hours to source and buy all the ingredients ;-)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: That’s not possible. I found everything but the coconut milk at Aldi.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
32g fat - count ‘em! 
Save time and just inject the fat into your arteries.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. I do suggest grating the ginger and using the full tablespoon of it. Grating the garlic helps as well if you want stronger flavors. My coconut milk is always solid at the top. I used all of the solids and then enough liquid to make 1 cup. I had no problem with too much liquid. The squeeze of lime at the end definitely brightens the flavors. I gave the recipe to a friend and she and her husband loved it as well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with ground pork - it was great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Totally tasteless until we sauteed mustard seeds until they popped then added onion with: cumin, coriander, turmeric, sweet paprika and cayenne. Could also use some peanuts and not very cooked red bell pepper. Now it's a keeper.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: roasted the sweet potatos (2) , threw in a chopped serrano, some fish sauce-- doubled the ginger-- halved the spinach (it gets a little slimy...) it was fantastic!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @cv I also added fish sauce and a dash of soy sauce — great minds…

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Followed the comments/advice to cut sweet potatoes in bigger chunks (and left on the skins!) also added a splash of fish sauce and soy for added umami and depth. It occurred to me as I made it that it’s essentially curry without the chili paste, so next time may add some green curry paste when I put in the aromatics. Mixed a little cornstarch to the lime juice when adding and it thickened perfectly.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Per comments I microwaved potato first for 3 minutes. Doubled ginger, added 2 Thai peppers and stirred in mint with cilantro- it’s a keeper!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Stupid good. I doubled it and used 50/50 chicken/pork. Made the recipe almost exactly as written - the only addition I made was a splash of fish sauce at the end with the lime and cilantro. This will be a weeknight standby!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I modified this recipe a lot and thought it was fine. I used ground turkey and substituted green beans for spinach, lite coconut milk for full fat, and fish sauce for salt and stirred in Thai red curry paste instead of adding hot sauce. The ground turkey had too strong a flavor for me. I will go with ground chicken next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Used ground pork and only had japanese sweet potatoes (closer to regular potato in terms of texture, not as sweet). Diced into about finger-width sized cubes. Love! def repeater.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Loved! I used a little more garlic & ginger than recommended and added red pepper flakes at this point for a little kick. Added a diced red bell pepper as I was browning the meat as I had one to use up. Added soy sauce & rice vinegar as the sauce was reducing down. I think that extra tang from the vinegar was needed. Used the rest of the coconut milk from the can to cook the rice and the whole thing came out amazing! My husband was eating leftovers straight out of the pan as he couldn’t get enough.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made it exactly to the recipe, (just seasoned the ground chicken a bit) and my hubby and I loved it!  I had leftover roasted sweet potato cubes that I added at the last minute which were yummy.  Next time I'd would throw in the whole can of coconut milk to have more sauce for the rice because there's no such thing as too much coconut. It would be delish with a firm white fish or diced chicken thighs.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: The recipe is really bland. Bump up the garlic and ginger. I also added a little fish sauce and it helped a lot.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I added red curry paste and fried it off before adding in the full can of coconut milk!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Skipped the scallions due to allergy and used frozen spinach. Added Costco Sriracha Seasoning and Chicago Spice House Chile con Carne Seasoning. Delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Very easy to make and very tasty! My family loved it!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I don't like cilantro. What could I replace it with? Or would it be ok to just leave it out without replacing?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @Sarah try a different flavored herb entirely, like basil. That would be lovely in this dish.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used 5oz spinach and a full can of coconut milk because that is the way they came. A large sweet potato and a 16-17 inch carbon steel skillet. A hit with the family. Delish!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: It was as quick as the recipe said, after all the chopping and prepping. Really versatile—used chorizo cause that was what was at hand, and some leftover chickpeas. It needed just a little something more, flavor-wise; I'm thinking maybe some heat, like smoked paprika.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Tasty and comforting but bland. The hot sauce made it delish, and definitely garnish with more scallion and cilantro.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was very nice and delivered on the name! I was a lot more patient browning the sweet potatoes, no need to rush that step. I also opted to cook the meat first, before adding the scallions and garlic. I went for the pork option and it was a winner. Served over basmati rice and garnished with cilantro and radish slices. Really good!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This dish was satisfying and easy to make. But I feel like I over-relied on the hot sauce that I served it with to provide oomph and flavor. Next time, I'm going to add two hot chiles (like serrano, birds-eye, or jalapeño) when I sauté the scallions and some soy sauce (or perhaps even fish sauce?) at the end.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This sounds delicious! Someone sent us some ground pork, can that be used instead of the ground chicken? Also, is substituting cubed butternut squash for the sweet potatoe ok?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @Missy I wouldn’t sub in butternut squash for the sweet potato, it would really change the texture and taste. And I love butternut squash! But with the ground chicken/pork, the starchier sweet potato is better

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Based on previous comments, I added more of everything: garlic, ginger, scallions, coconut milk, spinach, cilantro, except the amount of ground turkey, Unfortunately, this recipe was bland and uninteresting. Disappointing.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I had reduced fat coconut milk and coconut cream. Mixed them and worked fantastic! I liked it with a little more ginger. Made it in Dutch oven. We absolutely loved it! Easy and delicious!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and very easy.  I used coconut oil to up the coconut flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is awesome!! Absolutely love it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this on a icy winter evening and we all really liked it. I used onions instead of scallions because that's what I had on hand, and also added chopped orange and yellow bell peppers. I wonder how  it would be to add actual coconut? Very easy and fast to make.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Sweet potato overwhelmed everything just a little bit too weird

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Good! Easy. I added chopped, boiled green beans in place of spinach because that's what I had, and they worked just fine. Served topped with jarred jalapeno crisp, which went very well.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Is the "coconut milk" in this recipe the refrigerated carton variety sold next to cow's milk or the canned product in the Asian section?  Both say that they are coconut milk, but very different for cooking purposes.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This got a 6/10 from my family even though we all like the individual ingredients- just was not flavorful. Didn’t feel cohesive either. As I was cooking it I thought this seems like it needs more spices - should have read the comments! Hot sauce is essential but still not enough to make the dish good enough to warrant a spot in the rotation.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Quick and easy, esp. for a weeknight. Added peas too for more veggies.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Ready to prepare but not as tasty as I’d hoped. Kinda blah.  Will think about ways to boost the flavor.  “Fragrant” for sure in the delicious scent while cooking :)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night, it was outstanding! Made exactly as written with ground pork and the flavors and textures were great. The only thing I'll do differently next time is add a touch of heat -- red pepper flakes, or maybe some white pepper instead of black. I bet a wee bit of chili oil while cooking the protein would be great ... or at least a little chili crisp on top when served.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I realized I only have a can of coconut cream and not coconut milk. Can I use that instead?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: To be truthful, it was a tad bland.  I used ground turkey but perhaps using pork would give it more flavor.  In hindsight, I think some mushrooms would give it more depth.  I tried putting a little Crispy Chili paste for my seconds but that’s was all I tasted, the .

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Loved it! Added a splash of fish sauce towards the end.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used ground pork and put in a little fish sauce toward the end. Some sliced jalapeños as garnish are nice, too.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: After the sweet potatoes were almost cooked, I added sliced baby Bella mushrooms to the pan and they cooked with the finishing of the potatoes. It fit in well with the dish and added a texture and consistency that rounded off the dish perfectly. Otherwise, there was nothing very firm to pull everything together.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Quick and comforting, and absolutely DELISH! We practically licked our plates!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was really great comfort food on a cold winter night. healthy, too. The only thing I did differently was add some curry powder (yes Penzeys like the others) and the full can of coconut milk. It needs salt to make the flavor pop so I dont skimp. Otherwise, i could see that it could be bland without it.I also added a pinch of cayenne. Perfection

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Added shitake and chanterelle mushrooms that I needed to use, zest from the lime and a handful of cashews at the end. Perfection.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made with some reservations, but soooo good. A little fish sauce took it over the top!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
I made this tonight and it was delicious. Only things I changed:
Less spinach but added green cabbage 
Less lime (1 tbsp)
Splash of chili oil
Used whole can of coconut milk
Served with jasmine rice 
It’s a nice easy light healthy meal that tastes better than takeout!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: It is delicious but I found the texture too mealy, so I added a few handfuls of crushed pistachios to each bowl. The hot sauce is essential.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Home chef, but would not stand a chance on master chef. Followed recipe mostly. Added a shake or 2 of crushed red. Used light milk instead of full fat. My wife accidentally added the whole cup of sliced scallions… but it worked in the end!Overall, was anxious about enough flavor. Trust in adding salt as you go and taste.  Served over jasmine rice, a few more shakes of salt… couple dabs of hot sauce here and there. And I’ll be… it’s really actually quite crave-able. Didn’t think I would when I was doing it, but I’ll probably make this again.  (Thinking maybe I’ll add lemon grass next time)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This had great flavor. I used ground pork instead of ground chicken but followed the recipe otherwise. For ground pork, I'll skip cooking it in oil next time. We'll definitely be making it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Eh, …. It was underwhelming and lacked character despite amping the ginger and garlic.

Just overall , bland.
Super easy prep though.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Thanks to all the comments, I doubled the scallions, garlic, ginger, and cilantro, added fish sauce to the chicken, and used the whole can of coconut milk. Additionally, I used around a tablespoon of Madras curry, divided between the potatoes and the chicken. I made this choice because of my sweet potato: it was a white-fleshed Japanese sweet potato, and I didn’t want the dish to look so monochrome. But then I figured I should just commit to making this a true curry. I highly recommend this! Also, my sweet potato cooked just fine on the stove top, 9 minutes, but it was a true 1/4-inch dice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Very easy to make and tasty. I used Grape Seed Oil.
I would make it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this as directed and it was fantastic. The folks who found it wanting must have left out or cut back on the lime juice....it makes a BIG difference.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for dinner tonight. My tweaks this time: roasted the diced sweet potatoes in the air fryer and they got nicely caramelized, doubled the amount of aromatics and added curry powder and cooked the chicken in the meantime. I think next time I'll try with ground pork and maybe add a minced serrano chile, perhaps use some green curry paste.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Nice flavor but not incredible. A good way to get some veggies into young kids as this is something my 7 and 9 year old grandsons would willingly eat. But we won’t bother to make it again for us. Recommend a full 5 oz. of spinach. I also added a bit more of the coconut milk, as it was a bit dry with the recommended 1 cup. That being said, I used ground white meat Turkey for the protein, which may account for the dryness. Served with jasmine rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Thankfully this was an easy meal to make. Since it came out bland I wasn’t too upset. I even tripled the garlic, doubled the ginger. Had a few spoonfuls and then threw it out.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Enjoyed this recipe. Used raw small-dice chicken breast, and parsley, because that’s what I had. Added a little more garlic and ginger and more scallions. Frozen chopped spinach, and voila! An easy, quick, healthy weeknight dinner.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made it with beyond beef and it was pretty decent. I’m sure it’s better with chicken but wanted to try it since I had a pack that I needed to use. Definitely will try again with chicken

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Agree with earlier reviewer to additional garlic, ginger, and lime juice is needed. If juice has  collected with the sweet potatoes after they are removed, don’t put that juice back into the skillet. We found that the dish didn’t have a distinctive coconut flavor and it May have gotten diluted. Taste as you cook, and add in extra of spice ingredients and lime juice, to your taste.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: First time leaving a comment after cooking nytimes recipes for years - to say that this was the easiest and really flavorful and a total weeknight star.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used ground turkey and shallots as the only substitutions. There’s a lot of peeling and chopping with shallots, garlic and ginger. Otherwise it’s a quick and easy dish. I roasted the sweet potato chunks on sheet pan in oven to insure their doneness. Really delicious over rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: So easy and tasty. I like to think about recipes in terms of return on effort and this one gave fantastic results for the time I put into it. My kids asked for leftovers to take to school for lunch. I used ground pork because it was on sale and added a small splash of fish sauce.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious but also easily modifiable. Don’t skimp on the aromatics and lime. Added diced jalapeño, a bit of fish sauce and some chickpeas I had in the fridge. Didn’t have spinach but I didn’t miss it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used ground pork. Next time I would use half inch dice for sweet potato. I also used  half the salt and pepper. I enjoyed the flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Mildly Thai flavors. Easy to make. Don’t forget to add red pepper flakes.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made with turkey instead of chicken. Doubled the sweet potato since I had one. Wish I upped the ginger. Won’t be a repeat meal for me but glad to have something different tonight!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe. Flavorful, easy and leftovers!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Double the ginger and garlic. We liked adding crushed red pepper for some spice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  I used ground turkey and bok choy instead of spinach and added extra lime juice and some makrut lime salt.  The lime is the key ingredient that brings the dish together.  I made a double recipe.  Great for easy leftovers.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Truly a bland dish, even with the addition of red pepper flakes. There are better dishes requiring 20 minutes of chopping. Don’t bother with this one.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: BTW - the photo makes it looks more colorful than it is. Is that a yam or carrot? It’s not a sweet potato!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Cut a couple of chicken thighs into bite-size pieces instead of using ground meat, and subbed kale for spinach. It was very good but probably too much chopping to repeat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe. Absolutely delicious. Made as written. To make more friendly for my IBS, I subbed garlic vegetable oil for the canola and left out the fresh garlic. Paired beautifully with a Dr. Konstantin Frank Gewurztraminer. Will absolutely be making this again!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Fast & easy but very bland. If you choose to try this, I would recommend reading through the comments and adding some of the suggested spices like smoked paprika, pepper flakes or maybe curry.  I think there are plenty of other more tasty recipes on this site so I'm taking this out  of my saved file.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
No leftovers - so that is a good sign it was a hit with the family. I used a can of coconut (instead of just a cup) and put it over rice.

Caveat - the recipe above is bland! I added fresh jalapenos and hot sauce - and it tasted much better.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used diced chicken and it turned out great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  I did air fry the sweet potatoes first and used an entire can of coconut milk and let it simmer until the liquid thickened.  We only got 3 bowls out of this, so I plan to double the recipe next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: For vegetarians I substituted the chicken for corn and it was still delicious.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I did not have ground chicken, but had the other ingredients and was desperate to use my chicken thighs so just adapted it. (not on purpose but trying to use all my food before shopping again!)  I shredded the chicken once it was cooked/softened. I added ginger and lime to taste. We loved it over brown rice.  I will make it again as an easy, delish dish.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Super easy weeknight dish.  I began the sweet potato in an air fryer while I prepped everything else, then I browned them in the skillet.  Used a full can of coconut milk. Definitely needs a bit of hot sauce, res pepper flakes, or Serranos.  Looking forward to leftovers for lunch.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was okay. A bit bland as others have said. I added some fish sauce, which helped. Also, we topped it with Kimchi which added a good kick, along with homemade gochujang sauce.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe! New staple in the house. We prefer to use coconut cream instead of milk. Also seems to reduce evaporation time and flavor.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Really good and even better the next day.  Took comment advice to double garlic, cilantro, and ginger   Substituted ground pork for chicken.  Yum!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this yesterday evening and used ground turkey. I added extra garlic and ginger and the whole can of coconut milk per previous comments. I thought it was pretty good but my husband wasn’t crazy about it. If I ever make it again, I will used boneless skinless chicken thighs and try to think of something other than sweet potatoes to use. He really didn’t like the texture of the ground turkey and felt like the sweet potatoes made it too mushy.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious weeknight meal, exactly the kind of simple, uplifting, unexpected recipe I hope NYT publishes often and forevermore! I made exactly as directed and wouldn’t change a thing.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Definitely increase the ginger, and garlic if you like me. I would also use more spinach. Otherwise, this was easy and tasty and came together, including Prep time in an hour total.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Tasty and quick meal to prepare. I did increase the garlic, ginger & coconut milk & added a splash of fish sauce. Next time I think I'll add shitake mushrooms. Quick meals are great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: very good

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Would it be possible to make this with ground beef and regular milk and substitute the veggies and then freeze it?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @Just wondering you can do whatever you want!  Substituting the two main ingredients turns it into a completely different recipe, though.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I decided to use two sweet potatoes because I didn't think one was enough. One would have been enough. As a result, I overcooked the sweet potatoes because the pan was too full. When I added it to turkey, it made it a little too mushy. But the dish was still a good one!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Good. Added cumin to the sweet potatoes and extra seasoning. Used more garlic and green onion and easily an entire bag of spinach, as well as a healthy amount of red chilli flakes.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Our family really enjoyed this meal. My husband doesn’t like too much spice so this was great for him.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Deeply flavored? Nope. We found it bland, which is odd because mostly we find 5-star recipes deliver.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Really good.  Made exactly as written.  Like other users have noted, flavor was a bit flat or bland.  Wife’s comment was, “ This is a chic’s dish… or maybe a good post-colonoscopy dish”.  Lol.  Anyhow, some Gochujang fixed it right up.  Our rotation is a bit heavy at the moment, so a bit in-between if we’ll make it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and easy. I subbed dark ground turkey as that’s what I could get. No other changes except for a small amount of Red Boat fish sauce.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight and the family enjoyed it very much.  I usually make a recipe as offered, but had more ground chicken than called for and so I added (almost doubled) all the ingredients.  I had shishito peppers on hand and browned them w the sweet potatoes.  After reading comments, I also increased spices w red pepper flakes, fish sauce, and curry powder.  Honestly, we could have increased the spices a bit more….and will do so next time we make this.  But, we all agreed it was a “keeper” with tweaking it to the family preferences for seasoning.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I didn't read the reviews before I made the dish and I'll say that was a mistake.  The fact that it says "hot sauce, for serving" should queue you in on something.  Its very bland.  My personal notes would include, more cilantro and scallions both in the dish and as garnish.  Sriracha should be in there as well.  I can't agree with 5 stars as is.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Super easy and so delicious. Will be coming back to this one, for sure!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was...fine. It was easy to assemble and came together quickly but something about it fell flat. I won't be making it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this with ground pork instead of the chicken.  Excellent!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Strange recipe. Didn't taste bad, but everything about it was super weird.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Flavors were great. My wife and I both enjoyed this. Cooked exactly as instructed.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! Made as is (except used Turkey).

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: One of my favorite recipes I’ve ever cooked from NYT! Doubled the sweet potato, and serve with lots of lime and sriracha!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Like this recipe, but think it could use more flavor. I added some store bought tom kha curry paste, fish sauce, and extra garlic and ginger. Also used frozen spinach for ease and added beans to bulk it up. Yum!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious, didn’t change anything. Tops!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: My family doesn’t like coconut milk. Can I swap it for evaporated milk?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @Adrienne sure

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: @Sheila I use my food processor to grind cuts of meat and it works just fine. Cut the meat into chunks, 3-4 inch-ish, and pulse until you like the look of the product.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Agree, add more garlic, ginger and cilantro! Delish!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Personally, I needed to double the garlic and ginger and add some salt. Otherwise a perfectly fine one dish weeknight dinner.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: so bland

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I could make this once a week. We love it! We made it exactly as written.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Fast, easy, and delicious.  Followed the recipe but next time I’ll try it with ground turkey or ground pork, and I’ll double the ginger.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Not the prettiest but very tasty for sure! My 4-yo toddler had seconds. I had a bag of frozen leftover coconut milk that was slightly less than a cup but it worked out fine. Finished with a dash of fish sauce for the last seasoning

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Some commentors shared that the sweet potato took longer to cook.  Suggest making sure pieces are 1/4 inch cubes - which is smaller than you might think.  Mine were perfectly tender, golden brown, and delicious!  My 11yo loved this recipe.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This has become one of my favorite meals. So yummy on a cold night.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
I made this for my daycare lunch today. Most of the kids loved it (No hot sauce for them).

I used frozen spinach, and it worked well.

I'd double or triple the sweet potatoes next time.

The flavor boot from the hot sauce was wonderful!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: We thought this was terrible. Bland and almost a nasty baby food flavor. The dish almost made me gag. I don’t understand how I got so many positive reviews.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Making this a pantry meal. I plan to use shallots for spring onions, lean pork shoulder chunks for mince (longer cook time of course), double aromatics with chilli for flavour and swap raw sweet potato for roasted sweet potato quarters with an Italian spin (mixed herbs, garlic granules, onion granules, Indian Kashmiri chilli powder for mild chilli smoky flavour, celery salt instead of salt and cracked black pepper). Maybe swap coconut for evaporated milk if the coconut is too strong. Will report back when I’ve made it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Yummy, but mine needed more ginger, some red pepper flakes, and cayenne.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Might this work with bison burger?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was really yummy! Really great combination of flavors I would not have come up with myself!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This is a keeper. Easy and really flavorful! As per some comments I added extra garlic and ginger, a little lime zest, and a heaping 1/4t crushed red pepper. I dislike cilantro, so I subbed parsley. This recipe is officially in my recipe rotation!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used ground turkey and used less salt but added a splash of fish sauce at the table....delicious, especially on  a cold evening!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Lovely recipe, did salt/pepper/lime to taste at the end was needed. We also added chopped peanuts as a garnish which added a good crunch.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
We used ground pork but otherwise followed the recipe.   This gave us a very flavourful meal indeed, endorsed enthusiastically by all, including daughter-the-chef.   Two days later, the small amount of leftovers had a stronger flavour than when freshly made.
This will be a staple.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: awesome and easy

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this using already roasted sweet potato chunks - probably almost twice as much of that as the recipe calls for ( I had them on hand so I figured why  not). Definitely an extra bit of lime juice doesn't hurt, and we skip the cilantro because we're not fans. With the pre-roasted potatoes this literally takes 10 min to throw together, although I let it simmer for a while. It's even better the next day. My spouse requested that this be "in the rotation" from now on.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Easy and delicious. Used dino kale instead of spinach and just added it a bit earlier. Flavor was good until it was diluted by the rice; next time I'll up the garlic, ginger, and throw in a bit of lime zest and heat.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I was pleasantly surprised at how quickly this all came together, and I thought it was super tasty! Will definitely be making again soon. Start-to-finish took about 25-30 minutes, which is perfect for a weeknight, and there was minimal cleanup.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted duck for chicken and arugula for spinach because that’s what I had. Super tasty!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I agree with the recommendations to dice sweet potato in larger cubes and to add more minced ginger, garlic, scallions, and lime juice. I found the full fat coconut milk to be smothering, and I might try with lower fat or even with just some unsweetened coconut flakes sprinkled on top.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Used ground turkey, turned out great. Could easily add more veggies (carrots, turnips, etc). Suggest topping with some Sambal Olek or similar!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: A dash of fish sauce really set it off!!!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with tempeh instead of chicken as we are a pescatarian household. Added miso and some soy sauce for depth, otherwise followed the recipe and served with sambal oelek on top. Easy, delicious, perfect payoff for time committed. I loved the nuttiness of the tempeh and it made me consider adding a spoonful of tahini or peanut butter in a future remake….

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I used garbanzo beans instead of chicken. It was great!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
WHY IS THIS SO DELICIOUS?!?

Love this recipe. Quick, healthy, delicious, cheap. It is more than the sum of its parts. 10/10 make this dish! Extra good with black rice.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: OMG SO GOOD. Would do three potatoes next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: 4.5 - very tasty. Added a good hit of sriracha at the end to boost the flavor. Next time will add some Sambal Oelek in right before adding the coconut milk.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I never follow exact cooking instructions but did with this one as I was tired and lazy. SOOOO GOOOD!!!  Incredibly flavorful and so easy.  It will be on repeat.  And pretty enough and tasty enough to serve to guests.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: A little too bland for my taste - used a lot of hot sauce and wished I had more limes.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
I made this as written and enjoyed it far, far more when we ignored the “serve over rice” suggestion. I love rice but it’s not needed in this dish and really hides all the delicious flavors (plus there’s sweet potato). Unlike say a curry where the flavors are so strong you need rice to cut the spice, this dish comes out pretty well balanced right out of the pan imho. 

The first time we ate it (with rice) I kept wondering where all the ginger and garlic I’d added went?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I added a teaspoon of ground coriander for a flavor boost.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: not very flavorful

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: i added a TON of extra seasonings (cayenne pepper i think helped a lot) as well as extra garlic and ginger, i like chicken and sweet potatoes but i feel this def needed a bit more flavor :)

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was pretty good, if not special. From my take, and from the other comments, almost any protein and veggies would work along with the aromatics and coconut milk. I didn't have green onions, used shallots: didn't have chicken, used pork: had baby bok choy, not spinach: happened to have a sweet potato, but would have used carrots instead of making a trip to the market. I did add a splash of fish sauce and a bit of sugar.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and full of flavor. Do not skip the hot sauce! Next time, I will add red pepper flakes in with the aromatics. Perfect for weekly lunch meal prep - make a batch on Sunday for the week’s lunches!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I agree with others that this fell a bit flat as written, so added some splashes of soy sauce and fish sauce at the end, and more lime, and that really took it to "excellent" territory. Husband said "I would eat this every day."

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Just added red pepper flake to the meat while it was cooking.  I also used coconut cream because it holds the flavor so much better and I doubled the ginger because we like it.  Looking forward to lunch leftovers - thoroughly enjoyed this.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Very simple and lots and lots of flavor!  I did not want both potato and rice, so I added a sweet potato and eliminated the rice. I had a jalapeño to use, so I chopped it fine and added to the onions and eliminated the hot sauce.  Perfect with store bought frozen egg rolls!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Definitely needs fish sauce and a doubling of the aromatics. I will also brown the chicken next time first (not just cook the pink out) then caramelize the sweet potatoes first more flavour. Solid quick weeknight home cooking but wouldn’t serve to impress

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Definitely needs more flavor. WAY more garlic, ginger, scallions. My potatoes were larger so took 3x the time to cook.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I cooked this as written and really enjoyed it. I found the cook times in the recipe to be accurate, and I pulsed chunks of boneless skinless chicken thighs in the food processor to get ground dark meat. I will be making this again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: We thought this was delicious. Because I am a vegetarian, I used the Daring brand vegan chicken which has some spices added to it (paprika, ginger, mace, nutmeg, cardamon) which may have given it more flavor. Otherwise I followed the recipe exactly. Next time I will add more spinach however.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Think I would serve this as Shepard’s Pie, saving the sweet potatoes to mash then top the dish. Throw it in the oven and Bob’s your uncle.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with turkey sausage (because that’s what I had) over Farro flavored with vegetable broth. Outstanding, very easy to make, wouldn’t change a thing!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: So good- just use the whole can of coconut milk and used 2 sweet potatoes. Will definitely make again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written… except (yah, I know I know) I used ground turkey (thanks to TJ’s ….). Delicious!!! Served over orzo… I guess u could use anything to serve over, even more veggies.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Added juice of whole lime, whole can of coconut milk (large sweet potato), and 1T fish sauce. 5 stars.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was fine for a weeknight meal but nothing super special. There were no leftovers, so that's saying something, but it was a bit bland.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have ground chicken so I slightly thawed a package of Costco boneless, skinless chicken thighs, chopped to a small dice and used that instead. Per J Osborn's suggestion, added a few dashes of fish sauce at the end.  Delicious!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I couldn't find ground chicken, so I made this with ground lamb. It was absolutely delicious. I topped it with a little bit of chili crunch and ate it for lunch all week.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Followed the  recipe with the exception of adding a little more sweet potato and a little extra garlic. It was kind of bland. Tasted much better when I added some hot pepper post-serving. It was, however, very easy to make, and generally well received by my spice-challenged children.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was so delicious, I added some crispy garlic on top since it was a tad soft in texture throughout. The flavors are unreal and so easy to make.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I think this could have used a little bit more flavor/seasonings, and a little less lime, but otherwise yummy and easy!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This is really good.  I made the recipe as written and served it with rice.  Excellent.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made with ground turkey, and everyone had seconds.  I might add more ginger next time as it was a tiny bit bland.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for dinner yesterday. It took longer to cook the sweet potato pieces - 12-15 minutes. Added 2 tbsp Sharwood's Medium Curry Powder just after the ginger and garlic, stirring until fragrant, then added the meat (ground pork). Stirred in about 1/2 tbsp homemade Sambal Oelek just before adding the spinach. One cup coconut milk was plenty. May have used 3 tbsp lime juice (didn't measure it). Excellent flavor, but needed more salt - like maybe explicitly 1/2 teaspoon. Will make it again!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: The recipe was a good basic starter, but it needed a little doctoring up. I increased the ginger, added red chili paste and mushrooms along with the coconut milk.I loved how quickly it all came together

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Was good , used the ground chicken , but sure any protein would work … used entire can of coconut milk and extra garlic and ginger , also some lime zest with lime juice .  Hot sauce was good but overall tasty quick meal

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: What hot sauce?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Love this dish. Easy weeknight dinner. Be heavy handed with your ginger, garlic, spices, and lime. Throw in the whole can of coconut. Amazing.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with ground pork and used the whole can of coconut milk. The second time I added a medium cubed eggplant. I think it worked well. Using the small dice on the sweet potato only needed the 8 minutes cooking time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I thoroughly enjoyed this recipe. I used ground pork instead of chicken.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was very good, I followed other commenta and I added more spinach, and sweet potato I would definitelo make this again

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I served it to company, which was not a good idea before trying it out.  I increased the sweet potato, used frozen spinach squeezed out, and used the entire can of coconut milk.  I didn't have fresh ginger and used fresh parsley instead of cilantro.  I used gobs of powdered ginger and more garlic than the recipe called for.  It took longer to cook the sweet potatoes, and it ended up tasting like very mild mush.  Disappointing and I kept apologizing to my guest, but she liked it.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: We enjoyed this; we freshly ground chicken thighs using our kitchen aid attachment- having good quality meat is key.  We liked adding hot sauce and I zested the limes to use as a finish.  Might try adding some curry spice next time?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this based on what I had available. Used grpund turkey, lite cocmut milk, doubled the ginger, added some curry powder to give it some needed oomph, doubled the lime juice. I cooked greeb beans so they were tender, the. added them to absorb some of the flavor. The result was delicious and we'll make this again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: It does look delicious, though.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This is an easy and flavorful dish we really enjoyed. A lot of color and texture with sweet potatoes and wilted spinach. We added more sweet potato, garlic and ginger to taste. And we used ground turkey because it was in the freezer. This would also be good with ground lamb.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: How can this be only 3 carbs with rice and sweet potatoes???

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: First time making it, the market was out of sweet potatoes, so I used cubed butternut squash instead. I also used canned chunk chicken.  Delicious! 5 stars!

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Any ideas on how to lower saturated fat in this dish other than by diluting coconut milk with broth?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I rarely use full-fat coconut milk anymore. Instead I sub lite coconut milk that I blend with some soaked raw cashews. It makes a rich blend that is lower in saturated fat and works really well in most recipes. About 1/4 cup cashews per can of coconut milk is my go-to.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious quick and will go into regular rotation.  I made as recommended but added lots of extra fresh lime juice and Momofuku Chili Crisp.  I think cubed eggplant would go really well with the dish and will try next time.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: i followed the recipe exactly and found it very uninteresting. like the ingredients just didnt have much to say to each other. a surprising disappointment.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this last night, exactly as written. I would increase size of sweet potato, 1/4 “ turned to mush. tasty, and healthy, served with jasmine rice. I wonder how butternut squash would do as sub for potato ?

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: We loved this and will add it to the regular dinner rotation. I used leftover roast pork, diced. I imagine it would work with many different kinds of protein.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight and thanks to the commenters who wrote that the sweet potatoes needed to be cooked longer. Chopping them and putting them in the microwave first, with some water, before I cooked them in the pan, did the trick. I like saucier dishes, so I’ll add all the coconut milk next time. We had this with naan and jasmine rice and I’ll make it again.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made it with pork and thought it was bland. No matter, a topping of chili crisp fixed that.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: My wife and I love this recipe.  We even feed it to our toddler.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Very good! I followed other comments and upped the garlic and ginger, plus added a couple of dry, red hot peppers. I subbed ground pork for the ground chicken. Reheats very well. Wintertime comfort food.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: OK… I’m missing the hype on this. It’s good enough for a weeknight meal but not great or amazing. And yes I did follow the recipe, I didn’t make it with rutabagas and pork feet.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Super tasty! And great left-overs.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
I made this just now! I did have to use chicken thighs but that turned out fine. On its own, it was good but bland-ish. 

My experience: 
+ added a sliced carrot and some snow peas
+ added a bit of cinnamon, cumin, and some beef bouillon 
+ added a couple tablespoons of Korean pepper paste

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody:
Really enjoyed this. straightforward prep, one pan.

Keep an eye on the sweet potato, 8 mins was enough time to really soften them, so I was held back on adding them back in until right at the end (after wilting the spinach), or else they would've turned mushy.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I took advice from some of the comments and used 2 sweet potatoes, so I doubled the oil to cook them in and added more garlic. This one is a keeper.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for the second time. Such a lovely meal. Easy and fun flavors. The only change I made the second time was cutting the sweet potatoes a bit bigger.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: A delicious, healthy dish that does benefit from a little amplification. To me, bumping the aromatics is a no-brainer. Feel free to increase the ginger and the garlic. Also a no-brainer is FISH SAUCE. The dish skews Southeast Asian anyway. When the end-stage tasting process would normally have you adding dashes of salt, reach for the fish sauce instead, and leave Sriracha on the dinner table too. I found the "kicking it up a notch" process easy and fun, and the dish, a big winner in my house.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I suggest adding fish sauce, tumeric or a chilli paste. Without additional seasonings, it's bland.

author: Kay Chun
recipeTitle: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe. I used pulled rotisserie chicken because that’s what I wanted. I also used the whole can of coconut milk plus 1/4 cup water to make a little more sauce. I love one pan (except for cooking the brown rice to go with it) meals!

author: Kay Chun
